title: LangOS Semantic Vocabulary
description: The kernel's system call table. Every human language maps to these stable primitives. Applications depend on numeric IDs — never on symbols or surface verbs. Symbols exist for readability and debugging only.

categories.ACT: Actions — things an agent can do
categories.STA: States — properties, relations, conditions that hold
categories.EVT: Events — things that happen or change
categories.MOD: Modifiers — qualifications on actions or states

vocabulary.description: Bring a new entity into existence
Change a property of an entity
Place entity into a container or group
Enroll entity into a system or group
Take entity out of a group or system
Add a component to a system
Set a time for an event
Contact by phone or summon by name
Go to see someone or someplace
Be the right size or kind for
Question about meaning of a term
Question about length of time (how long)
Transition from one state to another
Something was released or started publicly
Moved to higher version or level
Moved to lower version or level
Is connected in some way to
In order to / for the purpose of
How the action is done (carefully, quickly)
Patient promoted to subject (was done by)
Ask for clarification (what do you mean?)
Self or third-party introduction (I am..., this is...)
Encouragement (you can do it, keep going)
Consolation (it will be okay, don't worry)

reserved_references.description: The person producing the utterance (I, me, my, myself)
The person receiving the utterance (you, your, yourself)
Most recent entity in context (he, she, it, they, this, that)
Most recent action in context (do that, repeat)
Past time reference (yesterday, last week, before)
Future time reference (tomorrow, next week, soon)

roles.agent: The deliberate performer of the action
roles.patient: The entity undergoing the action or being affected by it
roles.theme: The entity being described, moved, or discussed (non-affected)
roles.experiencer: The entity experiencing a mental state (know, want, see)
roles.instrument: Tool or means used to perform the action
roles.container: The group, class, system, or location receiving the patient
roles.attribute: A property or quality being described
roles.name: Label or identifier being assigned
